mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate reduces the discount rate on future earnings and supports equity valuation expansion, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not higher when FEDFUNDS trends down versus when it trends flat or up, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates above its recent trend, inflation expectations rise faster than nominal growth, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month moving average by more than 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day returns more often than positive returns, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the prior 3-month CPIAUCSL trend.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals deteriorating labor income and weaker household spending, so increases in unemployment predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-shock windows fail to show below-zero 60-day forward returns, or if the sign flips positive in a majority of 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening payroll income and retail demand, which improves earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint effect is not stronger than either series alone, or if positive 20-day returns do not occur more than 55% of the time when both PAYEMS and RSAFS are rising.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after prior weakness signals improving industrial output and higher cyclical earnings momentum, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month after at least two prior monthly declines
failure_condition: Reject if rebound months are not followed by above-baseline 5-day returns, or if the signal loses predictive power after 2-year rolling estimation.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and GDPC1 both improve, the economy is transitioning into a broad recovery that raises risk appetite and equity cash-flow expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-GDPC1 improvement does not predict positive 60-day returns better than either proxy alone, or if the effect is absent in recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, high or rising FEDFUNDS combined with accelerating CPIAUCSL raises real-rate pressure and equity duration risk, so the model predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is rising
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening episodes do not show negative 20-day returns, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power beyond FEDFUNDS or CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a shallow-growth labor market, falling PAYEMS together with rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and consumption, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises for 2 consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Reject if joint deterioration episodes do not lead to below-zero 60-day returns, or if the observed effect is fully explained by either PAYEMS or UNRATE alone.
